extracted_codes.context: Use available mapping to find all drugs/medication in the hierarchical Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) Classification. Diabetes Medications ATC 2nd level A10: DRUGS USED IN DIABETES

clinical_criteria: Measurements outside +/- 6 SD from overall mean HbA1c (statistical outlier exclusion)
Exclude measurements taken within 1 month (window: -1 month to +1 month) of an Inpatient or Emergency Department visit
Exclude individuals who have T2D Dx or anti-diabetic drug records prior to the measurement
Exclude individuals who have blood transfusion within 3 months before (window: -3 months to time of measurement) the measurement
algorithm_summary: The PRIMED Consortium HbA1C phenotype algorithm follows a 7-step process: (1) Extract HbA1c measurements using measurement codes from Hba1c_codes.csv, (2) Convert units to percentage using the formula HbA1c_% = (HbA1c_mmolL/10.929) + 2.15, (3) Select the earliest measurement per individual, (4) Apply individual exclusion criteria (end stage kidney disease, splenectomy/spleen disorders, T1D and other non-T2D diabetes types), (5) Apply measurement exclusions (age <18 years, measurements within 1 month of inpatient/ED visits, prior T2D diagnosis or anti-diabetic drugs, blood transfusion within 3 months), (6) Remove statistical outliers (±6 SD from mean), and (7) Flag individuals with T2D diagnosis or anti-diabetes drugs after the selected measurement for reporting purposes. The algorithm references external CSV files for specific codes: Hba1c_codes.csv for measurement codes, Hba1c_exclusions for exclusion criteria codes, and 'Drugs Used in Diabetes.csv' for ATC A10 medication codes.
